definition: Contemporary web/UI style using bold outlines, hard shadows, loud blocks, playful anti-polish, and high-contrast color.
notes: Use for modern colorful UI brutalism. Do not collapse with historical/raw Brutalism when the distinction matters.

definition: Raw anti-design style using stark structure, visible borders, default/plain type, asymmetry, and intentional roughness.
notes: Use for harsher/raw editorial or experimental interfaces.

definition: Reduction-focused design using whitespace, strong hierarchy, grid, restraint, and only essential elements.
notes: Use Minimalism as broad canonical term; Swiss Style is a subtype/reference.

definition: Frosted translucent surfaces with blur, layered depth, subtle borders, and luminous backgrounds.
Soft extruded interface style with subtle convex/concave surfaces and low-contrast shadows.
notes: Use cautiously because accessibility can suffer.

definition: Playful soft 3D style with rounded inflated forms, thick surfaces, pastel volume, and tactile feedback.

aliases: Aurora;aurora;Gradient Mesh;Gradient Mesh / Aurora Evolved;Glass Aurora
definition: Atmospheric gradient style using mesh/aurora color fields, glow, and soft luminous transitions.
notes: Use as visual atmosphere, not a substitute for hierarchy.

definition: Dark high-contrast sci-fi style with neon accents, HUD lines, mono/data typography, and glitch/signal motifs.
notes: Use sparingly for serious finance/health/public UI.

definition: Vintage sci-fi design language combining nostalgic space-age references with futuristic forms.
Nature-informed UI using earth palettes, organic geometry, botanical motifs, and calm spatial rhythm.
Accessible evolution of soft UI with better contrast, modern shadows, and clearer focus states.
notes: Prefer over pure Neumorphism for production UI.

definition: Two-dimensional style using solid colors, clean icons, minimal depth, and fast functional surfaces.
notes: Works well for utilitarian and cross-platform UI.

aliases: OLED dark;dark mode oled;Dark OLED;OLED Mode
definition: Dark interface optimized for OLED and low-light use, using deep surfaces and high-contrast foregrounds.
notes: Reserve glow/saturated accents for status and focus.

definition: Two-color treatment for icons, imagery, or illustration, usually primary plus secondary tone.
notes: Good for marketing and feature visuals.

definition: Named reusable design values organized as primitive, semantic, and component decisions.
notes: Use for consistency, theming, and maintainable variants.

definition: Purpose-based token aliases such as background, foreground, primary, danger, focus.
Component-specific token contracts such as button background, input border, card radius.
notes: Use for repeated component behavior and state contracts.

definition: Coherent visual concept expressed through type, color, composition, material, and motion.
Final packaging of code/assets/specs with naming, metadata, accessibility, dimensions, and approval notes.
notes: Use when producing deliverables beyond code.
